name: Chapter 3: Eight Years in the Physics of Consulting (1972–1979)
description: Peter Kastner recounts his eight years (1972–1979) at Arthur D. Little (ADL) in Cambridge, where he shifted from machine-level programming at Philip Hankins Inc. to broad-based consulting across banking, healthcare, manufacturing, utilities, and public safety. Through engagements at Chase Manhattan Bank, a Rio de Janeiro hospital, Continental Forest Industries, Wisconsin Electric, Hampton VA Police, Houghton Mifflin, and United Shoe Machinery, he developed a systems-failure taxonomy centered on organizational, political, and financial misalignment rather than purely technical failure. The chapter closes with Kastner’s capitalist awakening: watching the minicomputer revolution invert the economics of computing, he left ADL for Prime Computer, trading a coding pad for a market-shaping role.
